heading: Department of the Treasury Forfeiture Fund
label: 9705 Department of the Treasury Forfeiture Fund
notes: References in TextSections 7301 and 7302 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, referred to in subsecs. (a), (c)(2)(A), (g)(1), and (i)(1), are classified to sections 7301 and 7302, respectively, of Title 26, Internal Revenue Code. .The Bank Secrecy Act, referred to in subsec. (a)(2)(B)(iv), is title I of , , , which is classified principally to chapter 21 (§ 1951 et seq.) of Title 12, Banks and Banking, and has also been the popular name of provisions reenacted as subchapter II of chapter 53 of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. (g)(3)(B). , inserted at end “Further, transfers under subparagraph (A) may not exceed one-half of the excess unobligated balance for a year. In addition, transfers under subparagraph (A) may be made only to the extent that the sum of the transfers in a fiscal year and one-half of the unobligated balance at the beginning of that fiscal year for the Special Forfeiture Fund does not exceed $100,000,000.”Pub. L. 103–322, § 90205(c)(1)(B)Subsec. (g)(4)(A). , struck out “(i)” after “(A)” and struck out cl. The amount transferred under this clause shall reflect the Department of the Treasury’s pro rata share of the amount required to be transferred by the Attorney General pursuant to .”Pub. L. 105–61, title I, § 122(a)111 Stat. 1289Oct. 10, 1997“The Secretary of the Treasury is authorized to receive all unavailable collections transferred from the Special Forfeiture Fund established by section 26073 [6073] of the Anti-Drug Abuse Act of 1988 () by the Director of the Office of Drug Control Policy as a deposit into the Treasury Forfeiture Fund (), to become available for obligation on , as revenue available for purposes identified under .”21 U.S.C. 150931 U.S.C. 9703(a)31 U.S.C. 9703(g)(4)(B)October 1, 1998

children.label: A
children.text: Payment of all proper expenses of seizure (including investigative costs incurred by a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization leading to seizure) or the proceedings of forfeiture and sale, including the expenses of detention, inventory, security, maintenance, advertisement, or disposal of the property, and if condemned by a court and a bond for such costs was not given, the costs as taxed by the court.

children.label: i
children.text: contract services;

children.label: ii
children.text: the employment of outside contractors to operate and manage properties or to provide other specialized services necessary to dispose of such properties in an effort to maximize the return from such properties; and

children.label: iii
children.text: reimbursing any Federal, State, or local agency for any expenditures made to perform the functions described in this subparagraph.

children.label: C
children.text: Awards of compensation to informers under section 619 of the Tariff Act of 1930 ().19 U.S.C. 1619

children.label: i
children.text: liens for freight, charges, and contributions in general average, notice of which has been filed with the appropriate Customs officer according to law; and

children.label: ii
children.text: subject to the discretion of the Secretary, other valid liens and mortgages against property that has been forfeited pursuant to any law enforced or administered by a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization. To determine the validity of any such lien or mortgage, the amount of payment to be made, and to carry out the functions described in this subparagraph, the Secretary may employ and compensate attorneys and other personnel skilled in State real estate law.

children.label: E
children.text: Payment of amounts authorized by law with respect to remission and mitigation.

children.label: F
children.text: Payment of claims of parties in interest to property disposed of under section 612(b) of the Tariff Act of 1930 (), in the amounts applicable to such claims at the time of seizure.19 U.S.C. 1612(b)

children.label: G
children.text: Equitable sharing payments made to other Federal agencies, State and local law enforcement agencies, and foreign countries pursuant to section 616(c) of the Tariff Act of 1930 (), , or subsection (g) of this section, and all costs related thereto.19 U.S.C. 1616a(c)section 981 of title 18

children.label: H
children.text: Payment for services of experts and consultants needed by a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization to carry out the organization’s duties relating to seizure and forfeiture.

children.label: I
children.text: Payment of overtime salaries, travel, fuel, training, equipment, and other similar costs of State or local law enforcement officers that are incurred in joint law enforcement operations with a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization.

children.label: i
children.text: the purchase or lease of automatic data processing systems (not less than a majority of which use will be related to such program);

children.label: ii
children.text: training;

children.label: iii
children.text: printing; and

children.label: I
children.text: the identification of forfeitable assets;

children.label: II
children.text: the processing of and accounting for forfeitures; and

children.label: III
children.text: the storage, maintenance, protection, and destruction of controlled substances.

children.label: A
children.text: payment of awards for information or assistance leading to a civil or criminal forfeiture involving any Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization participating in the Fund;

children.label: I
children.text: a violation of section 1956 or 1957 of title 18 (relating to money laundering); or

children.label: II
children.text: a law, the violation of which may subject property to forfeiture under section 981 or 982 of title 18;

children.label: ii
children.text: the United States Customs Service with respect to drug smuggling or a violation of section 542 or 545 of title 18 (relating to fraudulent customs invoices or smuggling);

children.label: I
children.text: section 1028, 1029, or 1030 of title 18;

children.label: II
children.text: any law of the United States relating to coins, obligations, or securities of the United States or of a foreign government; or

children.label: III
children.text: any law of the United States which the United States Secret Service is authorized to enforce relating to fraud or other criminal or unlawful activity in or against any federally insured financial institution, the Resolution Trust Corporation, or the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation;

children.label: iv
children.text: the United States Customs Service or the Internal Revenue Service with respect to a violation of chapter 53 of this title (relating to the Bank Secrecy Act);

children.label: v
children.text: United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement with respect to a violation of chapter 77 of title 18 (relating to human trafficking), chapter 109A of title 18 (relating to sexual abuse), chapter 110 of title 18 (relating to child sexual exploitation), or chapter 117 of title 18 (relating to transportation for illegal sexual activity and related crimes); and

children.label: vi
children.text: the United States Coast Guard with respect to any law of the United States which the Coast Guard is authorized to enforce, assist in the enforcement of, or administer pursuant to section 102, 522, or 525 of title 14;

children.label: C
children.text: payment of costs for publicizing awards available under section 619 of the Tariff Act of 1930 ();19 U.S.C. 1619

children.label: D
children.text: payment for equipment for any vessel, vehicle, or aircraft available for official use by a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization to enable the vessel, vehicle, or aircraft to assist in law enforcement functions, and for other equipment directly related to seizure or forfeiture, including laboratory equipment, protective equipment, communications equipment, and the operation and maintenance costs of such equipment;

children.label: E
children.text: the payment of claims against employees of the Customs Service settled by the Secretary under section 630 of the Tariff Act of 1930;

children.label: F
children.text: payment for equipment for any vessel, vehicle, or aircraft available for official use by a State or local law enforcement agency to enable the vessel, vehicle, or aircraft to assist in law enforcement functions if the vessel, vehicle, or aircraft will be used in joint law enforcement operations with a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization;

children.label: G
children.text: reimbursement of private persons for expenses incurred by such persons in cooperating with a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization in investigations and undercover law enforcement operations; and

children.label: H
children.text: payment for training foreign law enforcement personnel with respect to seizure or forfeiture activities of a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization.
Any payment made under subparagraph (D) or (E) of subsection (a)(1) with respect to a seizure or a forfeiture of property shall not exceed the value of the property at the time of the seizure.
Any payment made under subsection (a)(1)(G) with respect to a seizure or forfeiture of property shall not exceed the value of the property at the time of disposition.

The Secretary may exempt the procurement of contract services under the Fund from division C (except sections 3302, 3501(b), 3509, 3906, 4710, and 4711) of subtitle I of title 41, section 6101(b) to (d) of title 41, and other provisions of law as may be necessary to maintain the security and confidentiality of related criminal investigations.

children.label: A
children.text: has a value that bears a reasonable relationship to the degree of participation of the State or local agency in the law enforcement effort resulting in the forfeiture, taking into account the total value of all property forfeited and the total law enforcement effort with respect to the violation of law on which the forfeiture is based; and

children.label: B
children.text: will serve to encourage further cooperation between the recipient State or local agency and Federal law enforcement agencies.

Amounts transferred by the Attorney General pursuant to , or by the Postmaster General pursuant to , and deposited into the Fund pursuant to subsection (c), shall be available for Federal law enforcement related purposes of the Department of the Treasury law enforcement organizations.section 524(c)(1) of title 28section 2003 of title 39

children.label: A
children.text: all currency forfeited during fiscal year 1993, and all proceeds from forfeitures during fiscal year 1993, under any law enforced or administered by the United States Customs Service or the United States Coast Guard;

children.label: B
children.text: all income from investments made under subsection (d); and

children.label: C
children.text: all amounts representing the equitable share of the United States Customs Service or the United States Coast Guard from the forfeiture of property under any Federal, State, local, or foreign law.

children.label: A
children.text: all currency forfeited after fiscal year 1993, and all proceeds from forfeitures after fiscal year 1993, under any law (other than sections 7301 and 7302 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986) enforced or administered by a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization;

children.label: B
children.text: all income from investments made under subsection (d); and

children.label: C
children.text: all amounts representing the equitable share of a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization from the forfeiture of property under any Federal, State, local, or foreign law.

children.heading: .—Investments
children.label: d .—Investments
children.text: Amounts in the Fund, and in any holding accounts associated with the Fund, which are not currently needed for the purposes of this section may be kept on deposit or invested in obligations of, or guaranteed by, the United States and all earnings on such investments shall be deposited in the Fund.

children.label: i
children.text: under any law enforced or administered by the United States Customs Service or the United States Coast Guard, in the case of fiscal year 1993; and

children.label: ii
children.text: under any law enforced or administered by the Department of the Treasury law enforcement organizations, in the case of fiscal years beginning after 1993; and

children.label: B
children.text: the estimated total value of all such property transferred to any State or local law enforcement agency; and

children.label: A
children.text: the balance of the Fund at the beginning of the preceding fiscal year;

children.label: B
children.text: liens and mortgages paid and the amount of money shared with Federal, State, local, and foreign law enforcement agencies during the preceding fiscal year;

children.label: C
children.text: the net amount realized from the operations of the Fund during the preceding fiscal year, the amount of seized cash being held as evidence, and the amount of money that has been carried over into the current fiscal year;

children.label: D
children.text: any defendant’s property, not forfeited at the end of the preceding fiscal year, if the equity in such property is valued at $1,000,000 or more;

children.label: E
children.text: the total dollar value of uncontested seizures of monetary instruments having a value of over $100,000 which, or the proceeds of which, have not been deposited into the Fund pursuant to subsection (c) within 120 days after seizure, as of the end of the preceding fiscal year;

children.label: F
children.text: the balance of the Fund at the end of the preceding fiscal year;

children.label: G
children.text: the net amount, if any, of the excess unobligated amounts remaining in the Fund at the end of the preceding fiscal year and available to the Secretary for Federal law enforcement related purposes;

children.label: H
children.text: a complete set of audited financial statements (including a balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow analysis) prepared in a manner consistent with the requirements of the Chief Financial Officers Act of 1990 (); andPublic Law 101–576

children.label: i
children.text: by property category (such as general property, vehicles, vessels, aircraft, cash, and real property); and

children.label: ii
children.text: by type of disposition (such as sale, remission, cancellation, placement into official use, sharing with State and local agencies, and destruction).
There are hereby appropriated from the Fund such sums as may be necessary to carry out the purposes described in subsection (a)(1).

children.label: A
children.text: $25,000,000 for fiscal year 1993; and

children.label: B
children.text: $50,000,000 for each fiscal year after fiscal year 1993.

children.label: A
children.text: Subject to subparagraphs (B) and (C), at the end of each of fiscal years 1994, 1995, 1996, and 1997, the Secretary shall transfer from the Fund not more than $100,000,000 to the Special Forfeiture Fund established by section 6073 of the Anti-Drug Abuse Act of 1988.2See References in Text note below.2

children.label: B
children.text: Transfers pursuant to subparagraph (A) shall be made only from excess unobligated amounts and only to the extent that, as determined by the Secretary, such transfers will not impair the future availability of amounts for the purposes described in subsection (a). Further, transfers under subparagraph (A) may not exceed one-half of the excess unobligated balance for a year. In addition, transfers under subparagraph (A) may be made only to the extent that the sum of the transfers in a fiscal year and one-half of the unobligated balance at the beginning of that fiscal year for the Special Forfeiture Fund does not exceed $100,000,000.

children.label: C
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall reserve an amount not to exceed $30,000,000 from the unobligated balances remaining in the Customs Forfeiture Fund on , and such amount shall be transferred to the Fund on , or, if later, the date that is 15 days after the date of the enactment of this section. Such amount shall be available for any expenses or activities authorized under this section. At the end of fiscal year 1993, 1994, 1995, and 1996, the Secretary shall reserve in the Fund an amount not to exceed $50,000,000 of the unobligated balances in the Fund, or, if the Secretary determines that a greater amount is necessary for asset specific expenses, an amount equal to not more than 10 percent of the total obligations from the Fund in the preceding fiscal year. At the end of fiscal year 1997, and at the end of each fiscal year thereafter, the Secretary shall reserve any amounts that are required to be retained in the Fund to ensure the availability of amounts in the subsequent fiscal year for purposes authorized under subsection (a). Unobligated balances remaining pursuant to paragraph (4)(B) shall also be carried forward.September 30, 1992October 1, 19923So in original. Probably should be “years”.3

children.label: A
children.text: After reserving any amount authorized by paragraph (3)(C), any unobligated balances remaining in the Fund on , shall be deposited into the general fund of the Treasury of the United States.September 30, 1993

children.label: B
children.text: After reserving any amount authorized by paragraph (3)(C) and after transferring any amount authorized by paragraph (3)(A), any unobligated balances remaining in the Fund on , and on September 30 of each fiscal year thereafter, shall be available to the Secretary, without fiscal year limitation, for obligation or expenditure in connection with the law enforcement activities of any Federal agency or of a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization.September 30, 1994

children.label: C
children.text: Any obligation or expenditure in excess of $500,000 with respect to an unobligated balance described in subparagraph (B) may not be made by the Secretary unless the Appropriations Committees of both Houses of Congress are notified at least 15 days in advance of such obligation or expenditure.

children.heading: Retention or Transfer of Property.—
children.label: g Retention or Transfer of Property.—
A
children.text: retain any of the property for official use; or

children.label: i
children.text: any other Federal agency; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any State or local law enforcement agency that participated directly or indirectly in the seizure or forfeiture of the property.

children.label: A
children.text: is one with which the Secretary of State has agreed;

children.label: B
children.text: is authorized in an international agreement between the United States and the foreign country; and

children.label: C
children.text: is made to a country which, if applicable, has been certified under section 481(h) of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ().22 U.S.C. 2291(h)1
Nothing in this section shall affect the authority of the Secretary under or section 616 of the Tariff Act of 1930 ().section 981 of title 1819 U.S.C. 1616a

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: h .—Regulations
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe such rules and regulations as may be necessary to carry out this section.

children.label: A
children.text: all moneys required to be deposited in the Customs Forfeiture Fund pursuant to section 613A of the Tariff Act of 1930 () shall instead be deposited in the Fund; and19 U.S.C. 1613b

children.label: B
children.text: no deposits or withdrawals may be made to or from the Customs Forfeiture Fund pursuant to section 613A of the Tariff Act of 1930 (); and19 U.S.C. 1613b

any funds in the Customs Forfeiture Fund and any obligations of the Customs Forfeiture Fund on the effective date of the Treasury Forfeiture Act of 1992, shall be transferred to the Fund and all administrative costs of such transfer shall be paid for out of the Fund.

children.heading: .—Limitation of Liability
children.label: j .—Limitation of Liability
children.text: The United States shall not be liable in any action relating to property transferred under this section or under section 616 of the Tariff Act of 1930 () if such action is based on an act or omission occurring after the transfer.19 U.S.C. 1616a

children.heading: .—Authority To Warrant Title
children.label: k .—Authority To Warrant Title
children.text: Following the completion of procedures for the forfeiture of property pursuant to any law enforced or administered by a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization, the Secretary is authorized, at the Secretary’s discretion, to warrant clear title to any subsequent purchaser or transferee of such forfeited property.

children.label: A
children.text: a judicial forfeiture proceeding when the underlying seizure was made by an officer of the United States Customs Service or the property was maintained by the United States Customs Service; or

children.label: B
children.text: a civil administrative forfeiture proceeding conducted by the United States Customs Service; and

children.label: A
children.text: a judicial forfeiture proceeding when the underlying seizure was made by an officer of a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization or the property was maintained by a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization; or

children.label: B
children.text: a civil administrative forfeiture proceeding conducted by a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization.

children.heading: Transfers to Attorney General and Postmaster General.—
children.label: m Transfers to Attorney General and Postmaster General.—

children.text: The Secretary shall transfer from the Fund to the Attorney General for deposit in the Department of Justice Assets Forfeiture Fund amounts appropriate to reflect the degree of participation of participating Federal agencies in the law enforcement effort resulting in the forfeiture pursuant to laws enforced or administered by a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization. For purposes of the preceding sentence, a “participating Federal agency” is an agency that participates in the Department of Justice Assets Forfeiture Fund.

The Secretary shall transfer from the Fund to the Postmaster General for deposit in the Postal Service Fund amounts appropriate to reflect the degree of participation of the United States Postal Service in the law enforcement effort resulting in the forfeiture pursuant to laws enforced or administered by a Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization.

children.heading: .—Department of the treasury law enforcement organization
children.label: 1 .—Department of the treasury law enforcement organization
children.text: The term “Department of the Treasury law enforcement organization” means the United States Coast Guard, the United States Customs Service, the United States Secret Service, the Tax and Trade Bureau, the Internal Revenue Service, the Federal Law Enforcement Training Center, the Financial Crimes Enforcement Network, and any other law enforcement component of the Department of the Treasury so designated by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Secretary
children.label: 2 .—Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Treasury.
